questions.question: A 55-year-old man presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to his back. Imaging reveals a large ulcer on the posterior wall of the stomach near the lesser curvature. Which artery is most at risk of being eroded by this ulcer?

A 43-year-old woman undergoes a splenectomy after trauma. During the procedure, the surgeon ligates an artery running along the superior border of the pancreas that gives off several branches to the stomach and pancreas. Which artery is this?
A 30-year-old patient presents with acute appendicitis. Which artery is primarily responsible for supplying blood to the appendix?
A 70-year-old man has ischemia of the distal transverse colon. Which major artery is most likely compromised?
A 25-year-old woman is found to have a congenital anomaly where the right hepatic artery arises from the superior mesenteric artery. Normally, the right hepatic artery is a branch of which vessel?
A patient develops an infarction of the jejunum. Which branch of the superior mesenteric artery is directly responsible for supplying the jejunum?
A 60-year-old man is found to have a blockage at the origin of the celiac trunk. Which of the following organs would be most affected by this blockage?
During surgery for colon cancer, the surgeon ligates an artery that runs in the transverse mesocolon and supplies the proximal two-thirds of the transverse colon. Which artery is this?
A 45-year-old man presents with rectal bleeding. Imaging shows a lesion in the upper rectum. Which artery is the primary source of blood supply to this region?
A patient has ischemia affecting the jejunum, ileum, ascending colon, and proximal two-thirds of transverse colon. Which artery is most likely affected?
